_comment: Ground truth annotation — CONSORT checklist for RED-CVD 2021 protocol article (cluster randomised diagnostic trial)

article.title: Improving early diagnosis of cardiovascular disease in patients with type 2 diabetes and COPD: protocol of the RED-CVD cluster randomised diagnostic trial
article.authors: Amy Groenewegen, Victor W Zwartkruis, Michiel Rienstra, Monika Hollander, Hendrik Koffijberg, Maarten Jan Maria Cramer, Yvonne T van der Schouw, Arno W Hoes, Rudolf A de Boer, Frans H Rutten

study_type.notes: Protocol article for cluster randomised diagnostic trial (RED-CVD); planned n=1300 patients in primary care practices; published protocol describes planned methods but results not yet available.

1a.notes: Title identifies as randomized trial: 'protocol of the RED-CVD cluster randomised diagnostic trial'

1b.notes: Structured abstract with Introduction, Methods and analysis, and Secondary outcomes sections

2a.notes: Background/Introduction provides rationale: early stages of CVD cause non-specific symptoms easily missed; high-risk patients (diabetes, COPD) benefit from proactive diagnosis; evidence-based therapies improve prognosis

2b.notes: Study objectives stated: 'demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of screening' high-risk patients with early diagnosis strategy; measure detection of CVD cases

3a.notes: Trial design described: 'cluster randomised diagnostic trial'; practices randomised to intervention (early diagnosis strategy) vs. control (care as usual); multicentre design

3b.notes: Protocol article does not describe changes after trial commencement; addresses protocol stage only

4a.notes: Eligibility criteria: patients participating in primary care disease management programme for COPD or type 2 diabetes; age 30-75 years mentioned in methods

4b.notes: Settings: primary care practices across the Netherlands; multicentre cluster randomised design

5.notes: Interventions described: intervention arm performs early diagnosis strategy during routine visits (symptom questionnaires, natriuretic peptide measurement, electrocardiography); control arm receives care as usual

6a.notes: Primary outcome: number of newly detected cases with CVD in both arms and subsequent therapies. Secondary outcomes: health economic analysis, quality of life impacts, adherence to diagnostic pathway

6b.notes: Protocol article; changes to outcomes would be documented in results paper if they occur

7a.notes: Sample size calculation: planned n=1300 patients (2x650 per cluster); power calculation described to detect differences in CVD detection rates

7b.notes: Data safety monitoring mentioned: independent data safety and monitoring committee established; interim analyses planned

8a.notes: Random sequence generation described: cluster randomisation method used; practices (not individual patients) are unit of randomisation

8b.notes: Type of randomization: cluster randomisation with stratification by region mentioned; allocation ratio appears 1:1 between intervention and control

9.notes: Allocation concealment: cluster-level randomisation makes concealment challenging; method for concealing allocation of practices not described

10.notes: Implementation: research coordinators at each site coordinate randomisation; practices receive allocation status after baseline data collection

11a.notes: Blinding: cluster randomisation and intervention nature (diagnostic strategy vs. care as usual) make blinding of practices impossible; patient blinding not feasible

11b.notes: Not applicable; open-label cluster intervention design

12a.notes: Statistical methods described: comparison of proportions between arms; analysis of covariance for continuous outcomes; intention-to-treat analysis mentioned

12b.notes: Subgroup analyses planned: stratification by disease (diabetes vs. COPD); sensitivity analyses mentioned for missing data and adherence

13a.notes: Protocol article does not include participant flow diagram; will be provided in results paper with enrollment, allocation, follow-up data

13b.notes: Protocol article; losses and exclusions will be documented in results paper

14a.notes: Planned recruitment period: 'will be included' suggests prospective enrollment; specific dates not provided in protocol but follow-up period through 24 months described

14b.notes: Protocol article; trial endpoint criteria not yet applicable

15.notes: Protocol article; baseline characteristics table will be provided in results paper

16.notes: Analysis population: intention-to-treat analysis mentioned; all clusters and participants randomised will be included in primary analysis

17a.notes: Protocol article; primary outcome results not yet available

17b.notes: Protocol article; outcome results not yet available

18.notes: Ancillary analyses described: subgroup analyses by diabetes vs. COPD; health economic analysis; stratified analyses by region and baseline characteristics

19.notes: Safety considerations: harms/adverse events will be monitored; data safety monitoring committee will review; no specific adverse events mentioned at protocol stage

20.notes: Potential limitations acknowledged in protocol: cluster design may introduce clustering effects; open-label design; potential contamination between arms; loss to follow-up

21.notes: Generalizability discussed: applicability to primary care patients with diabetes and COPD in Netherlands; tools used (ECG, BNP, questionnaires) readily available in primary care; generalizability to other healthcare settings noted

22.notes: Protocol article; interpretation of findings will follow results dissemination

23.notes: Trial registration: ISRCTN number mentioned; trial registered at international trial registry before enrollment

24.notes: Protocol availability: published protocol article in BMJ Open provides full methods; supplementary materials referenced

25.notes: Funding: supported by Dutch heart foundation and Dutch diabetes research foundation; funder roles in study design/conduct/analysis/publication stated as independent
